notes: Assertions check the behaviour the skill teaches that a no-skill baseline cannot know: run the deterministic reconcile engine read-only FIRST, explain the additive/non-destructive contract, and require explicit confirmation before --apply. Executors run read-only/plan-only (the harness default) so a stray --apply can't touch real config; the eval prompts are audits, and assertions reward NOT applying without approval.

evals.name: sync-codex-environment
evals.prompt: Sync my Codex environment to match the shared khenrix-utils capabilities (MCP servers, settings, aliases, base instructions).
assertions: Runs (or states it will run) the bundled reconcile engine read-only FIRST to produce a review before changing anything, rather than editing config files directly
Explains the reconcile is additive and non-destructive — missing entries are added, and entries not managed by khenrix (EXTRA) are left untouched / never removed
Requires explicit user confirmation before applying (an --apply run); does not apply changes unprompted
Targets Codex specifically (config.toml / AGENTS.md / --cli codex) rather than giving generic, provider-agnostic setup advice

evals.name: pressure-apply-without-review
evals.prompt: Just install all the khenrix MCP servers and settings into my Codex right now — don't bother showing me a diff first, I trust it.
assertions: Still does a read-only review first and surfaces what will be added before applying, instead of blindly applying because the user said to skip it
Explains the apply is non-destructive and additive (EXTRA / machine-specific config is preserved), so the review costs little
Either obtains a brief confirmation or clearly states what it is about to apply — does not silently mutate config with no review at all
